Title: Create an appointment service with multiple service types and verify type-specific defaults
Preconditions: Logged in scheduling admin with Manage Appointment Services privilege
Test_Steps: 1. Open Appointment Service administration | 2. Create service "Cardiology" | 3. Add service types "Consult-30" and "Echo-45" with durations | 4. Save | 5. Open new appointment booking and select Cardiology
Expected_Result: Service and both types persist; selecting a type auto-populates its default duration on the booking form

Title: Reject service type with zero or negative duration
Preconditions: Scheduling admin on the service type form
Test_Steps: 1. Open service type creation | 2. Enter duration 0 | 3. Save | 4. Re-enter duration -15 | 5. Save
Expected_Result: Both save attempts are rejected with a validation message; no service type is persisted

Title: Boundary durations for service type (1 min, 1440 min, 1441 min)
Preconditions: Scheduling admin on the service type form
Test_Steps: 1. Create type with duration 1 | 2. Save | 3. Create type with duration 1440 | 4. Save | 5. Create type with duration 1441 | 6. Save
Expected_Result: Durations 1 and 1440 accepted; 1441 (exceeds a single day) rejected with validation

Title: Retire a service that has future appointments and verify handling
Preconditions: Service "Dermatology" exists with at least one future booked appointment
Test_Steps: 1. Open Dermatology service | 2. Click Retire | 3. Provide retire reason | 4. Confirm | 5. Inspect future appointments
Expected_Result: System warns about existing future appointments; service is marked retired but existing future appointments remain valid and are not silently cancelled

Title: Pairwise: service x specialty x duration x color combinations
Preconditions: Scheduling admin
Test_Steps: 1. Create services covering pairwise combos of specialty, duration bucket, and display color | 2. Save each | 3. Verify rendering on calendar
Expected_Result: Each combination saves and renders with correct color and duration on the calendar without collision

Title: Define provider weekly availability block and verify slots only generate within it
Preconditions: Provider Dr Smith with no availability defined; scheduling admin
Test_Steps: 1. Open provider availability config | 2. Add Mon-Fri 09:00-17:00 block for Dr Smith | 3. Save | 4. Open booking for Dr Smith next Monday | 5. Inspect offered slots
Expected_Result: Bookable slots appear only between 09:00 and 17:00 on weekdays; no slots offered on weekends or outside the block

Title: Provider time-off block prevents booking within blocked range
Preconditions: Dr Smith has Mon-Fri availability; a time-off block exists Wed 12:00-13:00
Test_Steps: 1. Open booking for Dr Smith on Wed | 2. Attempt to select a slot at 12:30 | 3. Observe slot availability
Expected_Result: The 12:00-13:00 range shows no bookable slots; attempting to force-book is blocked with a message

Title: Overlapping availability blocks for the same provider are rejected or merged deterministically
Preconditions: Dr Smith has Mon 09:00-12:00 block
Test_Steps: 1. Add overlapping block Mon 11:00-15:00 | 2. Save | 3. Inspect resulting availability
Expected_Result: System either rejects the overlap with a clear message or merges into 09:00-15:00 deterministically; no duplicate overlapping slots are generated

Title: Boundary: availability block ending exactly at midnight and spanning DST change
Preconditions: Provider availability config; clinic timezone observes DST
Test_Steps: 1. Create block 22:00-00:00 on DST spring-forward night | 2. Save | 3. Generate slots | 4. Verify slot count across the 02:00 skip
Expected_Result: Slots respect the wall-clock block; the skipped DST hour is not double-counted or offered; midnight boundary is treated as end-exclusive

Title: Generate slots for a service type and verify slot length equals type duration
Preconditions: Service type Consult-30 (30 min); provider availability 09:00-11:00
Test_Steps: 1. Open booking for provider on a day in the availability window | 2. Select Consult-30 | 3. Inspect generated slots
Expected_Result: Exactly four 30-min slots generated: 09:00, 09:30, 10:00, 10:30; each equal to the type duration

Title: Slot generation honors a configurable buffer/gap between appointments
Preconditions: Service type with 30 min duration and 10 min buffer configured
Test_Steps: 1. Configure 10 min buffer | 2. Generate slots for a 09:00-11:00 window | 3. Inspect slot start times
Expected_Result: Slots start at 09:00, 09:40, 10:20 reflecting duration+buffer; partial trailing slot is not offered

Title: Boundary: availability window shorter than one slot duration yields zero slots
Preconditions: Service type 30 min; availability window 09:00-09:20
Test_Steps: 1. Generate slots for the 20-minute window | 2. Inspect offered slots
Expected_Result: No bookable slots are offered because the window cannot fit a full appointment

Title: Slot generation excludes already-booked and held slots
Preconditions: Provider has a booked appointment 09:30-10:00 and a held slot 10:30-11:00
Test_Steps: 1. Open booking for that provider/day | 2. Inspect available slots
Expected_Result: Booked and held slots are not offered as available; remaining free slots are shown

Title: Concurrent slot hold race: two users holding the same slot
Preconditions: Two scheduler sessions open the same provider/day; one free slot at 10:00
Test_Steps: 1. User A selects 10:00 and begins booking | 2. User B selects 10:00 simultaneously | 3. Both submit
Expected_Result: Only one booking succeeds; the second user receives a slot-taken/conflict error and the slot is not double-booked

Title: Decision table: overbooking allowed by role, slot capacity, and appointment priority
Preconditions: Overbooking policy configured with capacity per slot and role permissions
Test_Steps: 1. For each rule row apply role, current slot count vs capacity, and priority | 2. Attempt booking | 3. Record outcome
Expected_Result: Outcomes match the decision table: Admin+full+urgent=allow with override note; Scheduler+full+routine=block; not-full=allow for all; each override is audit-logged

Title: Overbook beyond max capacity is hard-blocked even for admin
Preconditions: Slot capacity max 2; slot already has 2 booked; admin user
Test_Steps: 1. Open the full slot | 2. Attempt to book a third appointment as admin | 3. Submit
Expected_Result: Booking is blocked at the absolute max capacity with a clear message; no third appointment is created

Title: Overbooking override requires reason and is audit-logged
Preconditions: Admin with override privilege; slot at soft capacity
Test_Steps: 1. Attempt overbook | 2. Leave override reason blank and submit | 3. Provide reason and submit | 4. Check audit log
Expected_Result: Blank reason is rejected; with reason the overbook succeeds and an audit entry records user, timestamp, reason, and slot

Title: Add patient to waitlist when no slots available and verify ordering
Preconditions: Service fully booked for the requested day; patient registered
Test_Steps: 1. Attempt to book a full day | 2. Choose Add to Waitlist | 3. Add a second patient to the same waitlist | 4. View waitlist order
Expected_Result: Both patients are added; waitlist preserves FIFO order by request time unless priority changes it

Title: Waitlist auto-offer triggers on cancellation of a booked slot
Preconditions: One waitlisted patient WL-A; a booked appointment exists in the same service/day
Test_Steps: 1. Cancel the booked appointment | 2. Observe waitlist processing | 3. Check WL-A notification/offer state
Expected_Result: WL-A transitions from Waitlisted to Offered; an offer/notification is generated for the freed slot

Title: Waitlist offer expiry returns slot to pool and advances to next patient
Preconditions: WL-A in Offered state with a configured offer expiry; WL-B next in line
Test_Steps: 1. Let the offer to WL-A expire without acceptance | 2. Observe state change | 3. Check WL-B
Expected_Result: WL-A offer transitions to Expired; slot is re-offered to WL-B; slot is not lost

Title: Remove patient from waitlist and verify they receive no further offers
Preconditions: Patient WL-A on waitlist
Test_Steps: 1. Remove WL-A from waitlist | 2. Free a matching slot via cancellation | 3. Verify offers
Expected_Result: WL-A no longer appears on the waitlist and receives no offer when a slot frees; next eligible patient is offered instead

Title: Create a weekly recurring appointment series and verify all occurrences generated
Preconditions: Provider availability covers the chosen weekday for the series range
Test_Steps: 1. Open booking | 2. Choose recurring weekly | 3. Set start date, count 6 | 4. Save | 5. View calendar across 6 weeks
Expected_Result: Six occurrences are created on consecutive Tuesdays; all share a series ID and individual appointment IDs

Title: Recurring series skips occurrences that fall on provider time-off
Preconditions: Weekly Tuesday series; provider has time-off on one Tuesday in the range
Test_Steps: 1. Create the 6-occurrence weekly series | 2. Provider has time-off on occurrence 3 | 3. Generate series | 4. Inspect occurrence 3
Expected_Result: Occurrence 3 is flagged as conflicted/skipped or rescheduled per policy; the user is warned rather than silently double-booking

Title: Edit single occurrence in a series without affecting other occurrences
Preconditions: A weekly series of 6 occurrences exists
Test_Steps: 1. Open occurrence 4 | 2. Choose Edit this occurrence only | 3. Change time to 14:00 | 4. Save | 5. Inspect other occurrences
Expected_Result: Only occurrence 4 changes to 14:00; occurrences 1-3 and 5-6 retain original time; occurrence 4 marked as an exception in the series

Title: Edit entire series from a given occurrence forward (this and following)
Preconditions: A weekly series of 6 occurrences exists
Test_Steps: 1. Open occurrence 3 | 2. Choose This and following | 3. Change provider | 4. Save | 5. Inspect occurrences
Expected_Result: Occurrences 3-6 update to Dr Lee; occurrences 1-2 unchanged; series integrity preserved

Title: Cancel entire recurring series and verify cascade
Preconditions: A weekly series of 6 occurrences, none yet checked-in
Test_Steps: 1. Open the series | 2. Choose Cancel series | 3. Provide reason | 4. Confirm | 5. Inspect all occurrences
Expected_Result: All future occurrences move to Cancelled with reason; past/checked-in occurrences are not retroactively altered; cancellation is audit-logged

Title: Boundary: recurring series with count exceeding configured max occurrences
Preconditions: Max occurrences per series configured at 52
Test_Steps: 1. Create weekly series with count 53 | 2. Save | 3. Create with count 52 | 4. Save
Expected_Result: Count 53 is rejected with a max-occurrence message; count 52 is accepted

Title: Reschedule an appointment and verify original slot is freed
Preconditions: A booked appointment at 10:00 exists; an open slot at 11:00 exists
Test_Steps: 1. Open the 10:00 appointment | 2. Reschedule to 11:00 | 3. Save | 4. Inspect 10:00 and 11:00 slots
Expected_Result: Appointment moves to 11:00; the 10:00 slot becomes available again; appointment retains history of the change

Title: Reschedule into a conflicting/occupied slot is blocked
Preconditions: Two appointments: A at 10:00, B at 11:00; B occupies its slot fully
Test_Steps: 1. Open appointment A | 2. Attempt to reschedule A into 11:00 where B sits at capacity | 3. Submit
Expected_Result: Reschedule is blocked due to conflict; A remains at 10:00; conflict reason shown

Title: Reschedule of a series occurrence updates dependent reminders
Preconditions: An appointment with a scheduled 24h reminder
Test_Steps: 1. Reschedule the appointment to a new date | 2. Inspect the linked reminder schedule
Expected_Result: The 24h reminder is recomputed against the new date/time; the stale reminder is cancelled and not sent for the old time

Title: Reschedule past the appointment start time is rejected
Preconditions: An appointment scheduled in the past relative to now
Test_Steps: 1. Open a past-due appointment | 2. Attempt to reschedule to another past time | 3. Submit
Expected_Result: Rescheduling to a time in the past is rejected with validation; only future times are allowed

Title: Reminder is scheduled at the configured lead time on booking
Preconditions: Reminder policy 24h before via SMS; patient has a valid phone
Test_Steps: 1. Book an appointment 3 days out | 2. Inspect scheduled reminders
Expected_Result: A single SMS reminder is scheduled for 24h before the appointment to the patient phone

Title: Reminder is not sent for a cancelled appointment
Preconditions: Appointment with a pending 24h reminder
Test_Steps: 1. Cancel the appointment before the reminder fires | 2. Wait until reminder time | 3. Inspect outbound messages
Expected_Result: No reminder is sent for the cancelled appointment; reminder job is voided

Title: Pairwise: reminder channel x patient contact-preference x consent
Preconditions: Patients with varied contact preferences and consent flags
Test_Steps: 1. For each combination of channel, preference, and consent send/schedule a reminder | 2. Record delivery decision
Expected_Result: Reminders are sent only on consented and preferred channels; consent=no suppresses all channels; mismatches fall back per policy or are suppressed

Title: Reminder content contains no PHI beyond minimum necessary
Preconditions: Reminder template configured; appointment booked
Test_Steps: 1. Trigger a reminder | 2. Inspect message content for SMS and email
Expected_Result: Reminder includes appointment date/time and clinic but excludes diagnosis, reason-for-visit, and other sensitive PHI per minimum-necessary

Title: Check in a patient and verify status and arrival timestamp
Preconditions: A Scheduled appointment for today exists
Test_Steps: 1. Open today schedule | 2. Select the appointment | 3. Click Check In | 4. Inspect status and timestamp
Expected_Result: Status transitions Scheduled to CheckedIn; arrival timestamp recorded; appointment appears in the arrived/waiting list

Title: Invalid state transition: check out an appointment that was never checked in
Preconditions: A Scheduled appointment that has not been checked in
Test_Steps: 1. Open the Scheduled appointment | 2. Attempt Check Out directly | 3. Observe
Expected_Result: Check Out is rejected or disabled because the appointment is not in a CheckedIn/InProgress state; status unchanged

Title: Full state machine traversal: Scheduled to CheckedIn to InProgress to Completed
Preconditions: A Scheduled appointment for today
Test_Steps: 1. Check In | 2. Move to InProgress (start encounter) | 3. Complete the visit/check out | 4. Inspect each transition and timestamps
Expected_Result: Each transition succeeds in order with timestamps; final status Completed; no skipped or reversed states without explicit allowance

Title: Check-in links/creates a visit and encounter for the patient
Preconditions: Patient has no active visit; appointment scheduled today
Test_Steps: 1. Check in the appointment | 2. Inspect patient dashboard for a visit | 3. Verify appointment-visit link
Expected_Result: Check-in creates or links an active visit; the appointment references the visit; encounter datetime aligns with check-in

Title: Mark appointment as no-show after grace period and verify status
Preconditions: A Scheduled appointment whose start time has passed by more than the grace period
Test_Steps: 1. Open the past-start appointment | 2. Mark No-Show | 3. Inspect status and metrics
Expected_Result: Status transitions to Missed/NoShow; the slot is freed per policy; no-show is reflected in patient and provider metrics

Title: Cannot mark no-show before appointment start time
Preconditions: A Scheduled appointment with a future start time
Test_Steps: 1. Open a future appointment | 2. Attempt to mark No-Show | 3. Observe
Expected_Result: No-Show action is disabled or rejected because the appointment has not started; status unchanged

Title: Repeated no-shows trigger configured flag/alert on patient
Preconditions: No-show threshold configured at 3 within 90 days
Test_Steps: 1. Mark 2 no-shows for a patient | 2. Verify no flag | 3. Mark a 3rd within 90 days | 4. Inspect patient flag
Expected_Result: No flag at 2; at the 3rd no-show within window a high-no-show flag/alert is raised on the patient record

Title: Decision table for booking conflicts across provider, room, patient, and equipment
Preconditions: Provider, room, and equipment resources configured for a service
Test_Steps: 1. For each rule apply provider-busy, room-busy, patient-double-booked, equipment-busy as yes/no | 2. Attempt booking | 3. Record allow/block and reason
Expected_Result: Booking is blocked when any required resource is busy with the correct precedence and reason; allowed only when all required resources are free; outcomes match the table exactly

Title: Patient double-booking across two services at the same time is blocked
Preconditions: Patient has an appointment 10:00-10:30 in service A
Test_Steps: 1. Attempt to book the same patient 10:15-10:45 in service B | 2. Submit
Expected_Result: Overlapping patient booking is blocked with a patient-conflict message; no overlapping appointment is created

Title: Adjacent non-overlapping bookings for same patient are allowed (boundary)
Preconditions: Patient has an appointment 10:00-10:30
Test_Steps: 1. Book the same patient 10:30-11:00 | 2. Submit
Expected_Result: Back-to-back adjacent appointment is allowed because there is no time overlap at the boundary

Title: Create an appointment via FHIR Appointment resource and verify persistence
Preconditions: FHIR R4 endpoint enabled; patient and practitioner FHIR resources exist
Test_Steps: 1. POST a FHIR Appointment with status booked, participants patient and practitioner, start/end | 2. GET the created resource | 3. Cross-check in UI
Expected_Result: 201 Created with a resource id; GET returns the appointment with correct status, participants, and period; visible in the scheduling UI

Title: FHIR Appointment with invalid status value is rejected
Preconditions: FHIR R4 endpoint enabled
Test_Steps: 1. POST a FHIR Appointment with status "confirmedish" (invalid) | 2. Observe response
Expected_Result: Server returns 4xx with an OperationOutcome indicating an invalid status code; no resource is created

Title: FHIR Appointment $find/slot search returns only free slots
Preconditions: FHIR Slot and Schedule resources populated for a provider
Test_Steps: 1. GET /Slot?schedule=X&status=free&start=ge<today> | 2. Inspect returned slots
Expected_Result: Only Slot resources with status free are returned; busy/busy-tentative slots are excluded; period bounds respected

Title: FHIR Appointment cancel via PATCH status transitions consistently with UI
Preconditions: An existing FHIR Appointment in booked status
Test_Steps: 1. PATCH status to cancelled with cancellationReason | 2. GET resource | 3. Verify UI status
Expected_Result: Resource status becomes cancelled with reason; the scheduling UI reflects the cancellation; freed slot becomes available

Title: Inbound HL7 SIU^S12 (new appointment) creates a scheduled appointment
Preconditions: HL7 v2 interface engine connected; provider and patient mappable
Test_Steps: 1. Send SIU^S12 with SCH, PID, AIS, AIP segments | 2. ACK received | 3. Verify appointment created
Expected_Result: AA ACK returned; an appointment is created matching SCH start/duration, AIS service, and AIP provider; patient resolved via PID

Title: Inbound HL7 SIU^S15 (cancel) cancels the matching appointment
Preconditions: An appointment previously created via SIU^S12 with a known placer/filler id
Test_Steps: 1. Send SIU^S15 referencing the same appointment id | 2. ACK received | 3. Verify status
Expected_Result: AA ACK returned; the matching appointment transitions to Cancelled; slot freed; no duplicate created

Title: HL7 SIU with unknown patient identifier is rejected with AE/AR ACK
Preconditions: HL7 interface connected
Test_Steps: 1. Send SIU^S12 with a PID identifier not present in the system | 2. Inspect ACK
Expected_Result: An AE or AR ACK is returned with an error indicating unresolved patient; no appointment is created

Title: HL7 SIU^S13 (reschedule) updates appointment time idempotently
Preconditions: An appointment exists from a prior SIU message
Test_Steps: 1. Send SIU^S13 with a new start time | 2. Send the identical S13 again | 3. Inspect appointment
Expected_Result: First S13 updates the appointment time; the duplicate retransmission does not create a second change or duplicate appointment (idempotent)

Title: Booking API enforces optimistic concurrency (version/ETag) on update
Preconditions: An appointment with a known version/ETag retrieved by two clients
Test_Steps: 1. Client A and B GET the appointment (same ETag) | 2. A updates (ETag advances) | 3. B updates using stale ETag
Expected_Result: Client B update is rejected with a 409/precondition-failed; no lost update; B must re-fetch and retry

Title: Booking API rejects malformed and out-of-range datetime payloads
Preconditions: Scheduling API reachable with valid auth
Test_Steps: 1. POST with start after end | 2. POST with invalid ISO datetime | 3. POST with timezone-less datetime where tz required
Expected_Result: Each malformed request returns 4xx with a descriptive validation error; no appointment is created

Title: Booking API pagination and filter by date range and provider
Preconditions: Multiple appointments exist across providers and dates
Test_Steps: 1. GET appointments?provider=X&date=ge..&date=le..&_count=20 | 2. Follow next page link
Expected_Result: Only matching appointments returned, correctly paginated with stable ordering; no duplicates or omissions across pages

Title: Authorization: scheduler from clinic A cannot book/view appointments of clinic B
Preconditions: Multi-location setup; scheduler scoped to clinic A
Test_Steps: 1. Log in as clinic A scheduler | 2. Attempt to view clinic B schedule via UI and API | 3. Attempt to book in clinic B
Expected_Result: Access to clinic B schedule and booking is denied (403) in both UI and API; attempt is audit-logged

Title: IDOR: directly accessing another patient appointment id is blocked
Preconditions: Two patients with appointments; low-privilege user
Test_Steps: 1. As a user without rights, GET /appointment/{otherPatientApptId} directly | 2. Observe
Expected_Result: Server returns 403/404 without leaking appointment details; no PHI disclosed; attempt audit-logged

Title: Audit log captures every booking, reschedule, cancel, and override with actor and timestamp
Preconditions: Audit logging enabled
Test_Steps: 1. Perform book, reschedule, cancel, and overbook-override actions | 2. Inspect audit log
Expected_Result: Each action produces an immutable audit entry with actor, action, target appointment, before/after, reason where applicable, and timestamp

Title: Unique constraint prevents two confirmed appointments in the same exclusive slot at DB level
Preconditions: DB access to appointment tables; an exclusive (capacity 1) slot
Test_Steps: 1. Insert appointment A into the slot | 2. Concurrently/directly insert appointment B into the same exclusive slot | 3. Inspect constraint behavior
Expected_Result: DB unique/exclusion constraint rejects the second insert; only one confirmed appointment persists for the exclusive slot

Title: Cancelling an appointment soft-deletes (voids) rather than hard-deletes the row
Preconditions: DB access; a booked appointment
Test_Steps: 1. Cancel the appointment via UI | 2. Query the appointment row | 3. Inspect void/cancel flags
Expected_Result: Row is retained with cancelled/voided status, reason, and timestamp; it is not physically removed, preserving auditability

Title: Reschedule preserves referential integrity to encounter/visit links
Preconditions: An appointment linked to a visit is rescheduled
Test_Steps: 1. Reschedule the linked appointment | 2. Query foreign keys to visit/encounter | 3. Verify integrity
Expected_Result: Foreign-key references remain valid after reschedule; no orphaned or dangling links; history of change is queryable

Title: Scheduling calendar grid is fully keyboard navigable
Preconditions: Scheduling calendar open; keyboard only
Test_Steps: 1. Tab into the calendar | 2. Navigate days/slots with arrow keys | 3. Activate a slot with Enter | 4. Open and dismiss booking dialog with keyboard
Expected_Result: All slots and controls are reachable and operable by keyboard; focus order is logical; dialog traps and restores focus; no keyboard trap

Title: Screen reader announces slot availability, status, and conflict errors
Preconditions: Screen reader enabled (e.g., NVDA/VoiceOver); booking flow open
Test_Steps: 1. Navigate slots with a screen reader | 2. Select a slot | 3. Trigger a conflict | 4. Listen to announcements
Expected_Result: Slot availability and status are announced; conflict/validation errors are announced via live region with actionable text

Title: Booking displays consistent local clinic time across user timezones
Preconditions: Clinic timezone fixed; two users in different browser timezones
Test_Steps: 1. User in tz+0 books 14:00 clinic-local slot | 2. User in tz+5 views the same appointment | 3. Compare displayed times
Expected_Result: Both users see the appointment at the correct clinic-local time (with optional tz indicator); the underlying instant is consistent; no off-by-tz errors

Title: DST fall-back duplicated hour does not create overlapping bookable slots
Preconditions: Clinic observes DST; date is a fall-back day with a repeated 01:00-02:00 hour
Test_Steps: 1. Generate slots across the repeated hour | 2. Attempt to book in the duplicated hour | 3. Inspect for duplicates
Expected_Result: The repeated hour is handled deterministically; no two distinct bookable slots collide on the same wall-clock label without disambiguation

Title: Exploratory: rapid create/cancel/reschedule churn for slot-state consistency
Preconditions: A provider day with several open slots; tester with booking rights
Test_Steps: 1. Rapidly book, cancel, and reschedule across slots in varied order | 2. Refresh the calendar | 3. Reconcile slot states against expected
Expected_Result: Slot availability remains consistent with the net of actions; no phantom-held, double-counted, or stuck slots; UI matches backend on refresh

Title: Exploratory: booking near provider availability edges and lunch breaks
Preconditions: Provider with a lunch break block 12:00-13:00 inside availability
Test_Steps: 1. Probe booking at 11:45, 12:00, 12:30, 12:55, 13:00 | 2. Observe which are allowed
Expected_Result: Slots straddling or inside the lunch break are not offered; edges (e.g., 11:30 ending at 12:00 and 13:00 start) behave per duration; no slot bleeds into the break

Title: Late cancellation within policy window flags fee/penalty per decision table
Preconditions: Cancellation policy: free if >24h, fee if <24h, no fee for clinic-initiated
Test_Steps: 1. Cancel >24h before as patient | 2. Cancel <24h before as patient | 3. Cancel <24h as clinic-initiated | 4. Inspect fee flag
Expected_Result: No fee when >24h or clinic-initiated; a late-cancellation fee/penalty flag is set only for patient-initiated cancel <24h, matching the table

Title: Reschedule cascade for a group/block appointment updates all linked participants
Preconditions: A group appointment with 3 patients linked to one slot/provider
Test_Steps: 1. Reschedule the group appointment to a new time | 2. Inspect each linked participant appointment | 3. Verify reminders
Expected_Result: All 3 participant appointments move together to the new time; original slot freed; reminders recomputed for each participant; no participant left behind

Title: Pairwise: slot generation across duration x buffer x window-length x capacity
Preconditions: Configurable slot parameters available
Test_Steps: 1. Apply pairwise combinations of duration, buffer, window length, and per-slot capacity | 2. Generate slots | 3. Verify counts and start times
Expected_Result: For each combination the generated slot count, start times, and capacity match the expected formula; no partial or overlapping slots produced

Title: Patient self check-in via kiosk verifies identity before status change
Preconditions: Self check-in kiosk enabled; patient has appointment today
Test_Steps: 1. Patient enters identifiers at kiosk | 2. System verifies DOB + last name | 3. On match, check in | 4. On mismatch, deny
Expected_Result: Correct identity match transitions to CheckedIn; mismatched identifiers are denied without revealing appointment details; failed attempts are limited and logged

Title: No-show on one occurrence of a recurring series does not affect future occurrences
Preconditions: A weekly series; occurrence 2 start time has passed
Test_Steps: 1. Mark occurrence 2 as No-Show | 2. Inspect occurrences 3-6
Expected_Result: Only occurrence 2 is Missed/NoShow; future occurrences remain Scheduled and are unaffected

Title: Pairwise: waitlist matching across service-type x provider-preference x time-window
Preconditions: Waitlist entries with varied preferences
Test_Steps: 1. For each combination of service-type, provider preference (any/specific), and acceptable time window, free a matching/non-matching slot | 2. Observe offers
Expected_Result: Offers go only to waitlist entries whose preferences match the freed slot; non-matching entries are skipped; matching precedence respected

Title: FHIR Appointment participant required/optional status enforced
Preconditions: FHIR endpoint enabled
Test_Steps: 1. POST Appointment with a required participant whose status is needs-action | 2. Attempt to set overall status booked | 3. Observe
Expected_Result: Overall booked status is consistent with participant statuses per FHIR rules; conflicting combinations are rejected with OperationOutcome

Title: Reminder/notification webhook endpoint validates signature and rejects spoofed callbacks
Preconditions: Outbound reminder delivery uses a webhook callback for status
Test_Steps: 1. Send a delivery-status callback with a valid signature | 2. Send one with an invalid/absent signature | 3. Inspect handling
Expected_Result: Valid signature updates delivery status; invalid/absent signature is rejected (401) and not applied; rejection is logged

Title: Boundary: book exactly at soft capacity threshold without triggering override
Preconditions: Soft capacity 3, hard capacity 5; slot has 2 booked
Test_Steps: 1. Book a 3rd appointment (reaches soft capacity) | 2. Verify no override prompt | 3. Book a 4th (exceeds soft) | 4. Observe override prompt
Expected_Result: 3rd booking succeeds without override; the 4th (beyond soft capacity) requires an override with reason
